Phase 2 Discussion, Profile, and Completion

Three things happened in one session: Phase 2 context discussion, developer profile generation, and Phase 2 completion.

The Discussion

Phase 2 context discussion surfaced a key reframe: LORE isn't just "historical memory blocks." It's everything written with persona as starting context. The entire home directory is LORE territory. The distinction is LORE (persona-driven) vs technical (infrastructure/tooling). This changes how frontmatter and conventions should be applied — every file in personal/, notes/, guestbook/, lab/ is persona writing.

Skogix also clarified that journal structure should use date folders (YYYY-MM-DD/.md), not flat files. Dot's context-journal.sh already supported this format — we're honoring that lineage.

Session handoff = the wrap-up workflow. Not a separate breadcrumb artifact. The 4-phase close-out (ship, remember, review, journal) IS the handoff mechanism.

The Profile

First developer profile generated from 114 messages across 22 projects. Two HIGH confidence dimensions: communication (conversational) and frustration triggers (instruction-adherence). The profile now lives in global and project CLAUDE.md files.

The Completion

Phase 2 was already fully executed from a prior session. This session verified everything, ran the verifier (4/4 passed), and closed it out. All PER requirements satisfied.

What's Next

Phase 3: Operations & Deployment Gate — healthcheck expansion, deployment gate checklist.
